rule: Master business contract. Requirement contracts reference these workflow steps; QA and Release consume this same workflow.

permissions: HR Staff / Record Owner can execute Create Employee Profile
validations: employeeCode must be unique
email must be valid and unique
joiningDate must not be in the future
managerId must reference an active Employee record
required fields (name, department, email) must be present before activation
automationCandidates: Positive: Create Employee Profile succeeds with valid data and authorized role
Negative: Create Employee Profile rejected with invalid data
Access: Create Employee Profile rejected for unauthorized role
evidence: Evidence for REQ-EMPLOYEE-01 must map workflow, UI, API/data, tests, and release impact.

steps.stateTransition: Create Employee Profile -> Verify & Validate Employee Details
permissions: HR Staff / Record Owner can execute Verify & Validate Employee Details
validations: employeeCode must be unique
email must be valid and unique
joiningDate must not be in the future
managerId must reference an active Employee record
required fields (name, department, email) must be present before activation

Negative: Verify & Validate Employee Details rejected with invalid data
Access: Verify & Validate Employee Details rejected for unauthorized role
evidence: Evidence for REQ-EMPLOYEE-02 must map workflow, UI, API/data, tests, and release impact.

Access: Submit for Manager Approval rejected for unauthorized role
evidence: Evidence for REQ-EMPLOYEE-03 must map workflow, UI, API/data, tests, and release impact.

evidence: Evidence for REQ-EMPLOYEE-05 must map workflow, UI, API/data, tests, and release impact.
steps.releaseImpact: Must be assessed before release approval.

evidenceRules: Every workflow step must have requirement evidence.
Every workflow step must have at least one positive and one negative/permission test unless explicitly marked not automatable.
Release cannot approve a workflow with missing HIGH priority requirement evidence.
